Title: Engineering a microbial biosynthesis platform for de novo production of tropane alkaloids

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamine required for ribosome biogenesis and mRNA translation; precursor to tropane alkaloids.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: native gene disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Polyamine-induced negative feedback during normal growth
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption relieves antizyme-mediated inhibition of ODC and strongly increases putrescine accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-mediated nonsense mutation disruption; combination with pathway overexpression; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A powerful engineering target to deregulate polyamine biosynthesis and boost precursor supply in yeast cell factories.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. OAZ1 is a key native negative regulator restricting putrescine biosynthesis. 2. Its disruption caused a strong increase in putrescine, including a 21-fold increase without pathway overexpression. 3. Combining oaz1 disruption with other engineering steps contributed to 71-fold putrescine overproduction.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Classified as Common_Gene rather than regulatory TF because it is an antizyme protein controlling enzyme activity rather than transcription.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Polyamine precursor for tropane alkaloid biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: native gene disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: native gene disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: dcSAM/MTA recycling context during polyamine biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Disruption increases putrescine production in the native Spe1-dependent pathway by reducing dcSAM-dependent alkylation pressure.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-mediated nonsense mutation disruption; pathway overexpression; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential SAM recycling concerns were tested; MEU1 disruption did not inhibit AbPMT1-mediated putrescine N-methylation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis-engineering target to improve putrescine precursor supply while remaining compatible with downstream PMT activity.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MEU1 disruption improved putrescine titer by 68% when SPE1 was overexpressed. 2. Together with OAZ1 disruption it helped construct the high-putrescine chassis. 3. Despite its role in SAM recycling, MEU1 disruption did not block AbPMT1 activity.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts indirectly through polyamine and dcSAM metabolism rather than catalyzing TA-specific chemistry.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immediate precursor pool for tropinone/tropine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: native gene disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: native gene disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Interpreted in context of MPO expression and 4MAB side oxidation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Native HFD1 oxidizes 4MAB to 4MAB acid, diverting flux away from NMPy; disruption reduces side-product formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-mediated disruption; LC-MS/MS quantification of 4MAB acid, 4MAB, and NMPy
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Part of redundant aldehyde dehydrogenase network; single disruption had modest effect.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Should be removed together with ALD genes to minimize aldehyde sink reactions and improve NMPy production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HFD1 contributed to oxidation of 4MAB into dead-end 4MAB acid. 2. Single disruption modestly lowered side product. 3. Combined disruption with ALD genes was essential for high NMPy production.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Contribution was only partial, indicating redundancy with ALD2-6.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Immediate precursor pool for tropinone/tropine biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: native genes disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: native genes disrupted in engineered yeast
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Interpreted in context of MPO-derived 4MAB and acetate auxotrophy upon extensive ALD disruption
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: These endogenous ALDs oxidize 4MAB to 4MAB acid; eliminating ALD activity increases 4MAB and NMPy titers.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Sequential single, quadruple, and ALD-null disruptions; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Extensive ALD disruption caused acetate auxotrophy; ALD6 reconstitution later improved overall tropine flux and growth/metabolism.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important chassis edits to block competing aldehyde oxidation, but must balance growth/acetate metabolism penalties; selective reconstitution such as ALD6 may be beneficial later.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ALD4-6 and HFD1 together accounted for part of 4MAB acid formation, but not all. 2. Removing ALD2 and ALD3 in addition eliminated detectable 4MAB acid and increased NMPy by 76%. 3. ALD6 reconstitution in the tropine platform improved tropine titer despite increasing hygrine, indicating metabolic tradeoffs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Grouped because the paper functionally treats these ALDs as a redundant endogenous side-reaction module.

Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive genomic reconstitution in engineered yeast; exact promoter not specified
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: native gene reconstituted after prior deletion
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: genomically restored in engineered yeast strain CSY1249
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25 °C; interpreted in context of acetate auxotrophy and NADPH generation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reconstitution of ALD6 relieved acetate auxotrophy and increased tropine titer 2.7-fold despite some increase in hygrine.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic reconstitution into optimized strain; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Also increased hygrine 1.6-fold while improving overall pathway flux.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Illustrates need for balanced chassis engineering: removal of side reactions can hurt cofactor and growth state, so partial restoration of key host metabolism genes may improve production.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Although ALD6 contributed to the undesired 4MAB acid sink earlier, restoring ALD6 later improved tropine output. 2. ALD6 reconstitution increased tropine from 565 μg/L to 1.5 mg/L. 3. Benefit likely reflected improved acetate metabolism and NADPH supply rather than direct pathway chemistry.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This gene had dual context-dependent roles: detrimental for NMPy retention but beneficial for final tropine production.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: putrescine to N-methylputrescine branch shared with nicotine/tropane precursor pathway
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from SAM to putrescine to form N-methylputrescine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropane alkaloid intermediate for anticholinergic compounds such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine used to treat neurological and other disorders.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a pathway bottleneck/rate-limiting step in engineered yeast; extra copies increased downstream intermediates and more than doubled tropine titer.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoters on low-copy plasmids and genomic integration cassettes; specific promoter identities not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25–30 °C; selective/non-selective defined media; glycerol supplementation improved final tropine production context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes flux from putrescine into N-methylputrescine/N-methylpyrrolinium and increases tropine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered yeast; stepwise pathway reconstruction; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; copy-number increase/bottleneck analysis
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Insufficient PMT activity limited pathway flux; some precursor leakage to side metabolism remained.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering target for increasing tropane alkaloid production; additional copies or improved orthologs/cofactor balancing should be included in pathway optimization.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPMT1 enabled conversion of putrescine toward N-methylpyrrolinium in engineered yeast. 2. In the optimized tropine platform, PMT activity was a major bottleneck. 3. Additional copies of AbPMT1, together with pathway optimization, substantially increased downstream intermediate and tropine titers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second PMT ortholog (DsPMT1) was later added with AbPMT1 to optimize flux, but AbPMT1 was the primary experimentally characterized PMT in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: JNVS_scaffold_2009311-derived ortholog; exact engineered construct accession NA
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: N-methylputrescine oxidase 1, C-terminally truncated without PTS1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively deaminates N-methylputrescine to 4-methylaminobutanal, which then spontaneously cyclizes to N-methylpyrrolinium.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropane alkaloid intermediate for anticholinergic compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Improved ortholog/truncation increased NMPy production, but extra copy later did not further increase tropine, so not established as final major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoters on low-copy plasmids and genomic integration cassettes; specific promoter identities not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Engineered variant tested for altered subcellular localization; original MPOs associated with peroxisomal targeting, truncated form lacks C-terminal PTS1
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Discussion notes variant identified from leaf transcriptome rather than root
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25–30 °C; effect interpreted together with ALD-null background to prevent 4MAB oxidation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves conversion of N-methylputrescine to N-methylpyrrolinium and increases NMPy titer.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog mining from transcriptomes; truncation engineering; heterologous expression in yeast; LC-MS/MS quantification of 4MAB, 4MAB acid, and NMPy
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome mining from 1000 Plants Project for ortholog identification; accession not provided for dataset in text
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product 4MAB was diverted to 4MAB acid by endogenous ALDs; leaf-derived ortholog may be suboptimal for root-associated TA pathway flux.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful engineering target for improving NMPy precursor supply; root-expressed orthologs with higher catalytic efficiency may perform better.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Replacing NtMPO1 with truncated DmMPO1ΔC-PTS1 increased 4MAB and NMPy titers. 2. Endogenous yeast ALDs diverted the MPO product 4MAB to 4MAB acid, masking full benefit. 3. In an ALD-null background, the optimized NMPy-producing strain reached 40 mg/L NMPy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: NMPy forms spontaneously from 4MAB; the gene acts upstream of a non-enzymatic cyclization step.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses N-methylpyrrolinium with two malonyl-CoA units to form MPOB.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropane alkaloid intermediate for anticholinergic compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a flux-limiting step; an additional copy increased tropine titer by 26%.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoters on plasmids and by genomic integration; exact promoters not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed according to prior discovery cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25–30 °C; glycerol supplementation and culture density influenced final tropine production context
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables formation of MPOB and supports tropinone/tropine biosynthesis; extra expression increases tropine titers.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous pathway reconstruction in yeast; plasmid versus genomic expression; copy-number testing; LC-MS/MS measurement of MPOB, tropinone, tropine, and hygrine
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AbCYP82M3 functional coupling inferred in pathway
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: MPOB can spontaneously decarboxylate to hygrine, creating a major side product; malonyl-CoA availability may limit activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical enzyme for rebuilding the tropane scaffold in heterologous hosts; should be paired with improved malonyl-CoA supply and tight coupling to CYP82M3.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPYKS enabled conversion of NMPy toward the bicyclic tropane scaffold intermediate MPOB. 2. Extra AbPYKS copy increased tropine titer, indicating partial pathway limitation at this step. 3. MPOB generated by AbPYKS also contributed to hygrine side-product formation via spontaneous chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The study infers that aspects other than expression, such as malonyl-CoA supply or intrinsic catalytic efficiency, may further limit flux.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidation/cyclization of MPOB to tropinone via hydroxylation and dehydration-associated ring closure.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropane alkaloid intermediate for anticholinergic compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Primary bottleneck in initial tropine reconstruction; substantial MPOB remained unconsumed, implicating CYP82M3 as a major bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoters on low-copy plasmids and genomic integration cassettes; exact promoter names not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Root-expressed according to prior discovery cited in paper
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25–30 °C; genomic integration improved performance relative to plasmid expression; oxygen transfer and glycerol supplementation relevant to P450 performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes a key ring-closing step to tropinone and strongly influences tropine output.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression with CPR partners; plasmid versus genomic integration comparison; LC-MS/MS measurement of MPOB, tropinone, tropine, and hygrine
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtATR1; EcCPR; PsCPR; Ncp1 tested as redox partners
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Plasmid overexpression imposes ER burden; incomplete conversion of MPOB leads to side-product hygrine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical target for pathway optimization; stable genomic expression, improved electron transfer, and enzyme colocalization may improve industrial tropane production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbCYP82M3 completed the second ring closure from MPOB to tropinone in yeast. 2. Initial pathway tests identified CYP82M3 as a major bottleneck because MPOB accumulated. 3. Genomic integration with AtATR1 markedly increased tropine production compared with plasmid expression.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Additional plasmid copy later showed little benefit in CSY1249, suggesting that earlier integration and context-dependent expression stability were more important than simple copy increase.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: P450 electron transfer support for tropinone synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers electrons from NADPH to the P450 enzyme AbCYP82M3 to support tropinone synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Medicinal tropane alkaloid intermediate for anticholinergic compounds.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not itself established as rate-limiting, but selected as effective CPR partner and used in optimized strain.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoters on low-copy plasmids and genomic integration cassettes; exact promoters not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Genomic integration and glycerol supplementation/NADPH balance affected interpretation of pathway performance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports AbCYP82M3 activity and enables tropinone/tropine production in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Comparison of four CPR partners in engineered yeast; genomic integration into optimized strain; LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful redox partner for engineering plant P450-dependent alkaloid pathways in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtATR1 functionally partnered with AbCYP82M3 in yeast. 2. Tropine could be produced with multiple CPRs, but AtATR1 was chosen for the optimized integrated strain. 3. Stable genomic expression of AtATR1 together with AbPYKS and AbCYP82M3 increased tropine titers.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts as an enabling support enzyme rather than a direct scaffold-forming catalyst.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Key intermediate for medicinal tropane alkaloids and related alkaloid derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established as major bottleneck; extra copy had no significant effect on intermediate levels.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Constitutive promoter via genomic integration and plasmid expression tests; exact promoter names not fully specified in text
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 25–30 °C; NADPH balance discussed as relevant to final reduction step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes conversion of tropinone to tropine in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genomic integration into NMPy-producing strain; pathway reconstruction with LC-MS/MS detection of tropinone and tropine; extra-copy test
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No major benefit from extra copy suggests other upstream bottlenecks dominate.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential terminal enzyme for tropine production; current evidence suggests optimization should focus upstream before increasing TR1 dosage.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DsTR1 enabled final reduction of tropinone to tropine in yeast. 2. Its integration was necessary for tropine production. 3. Additional copy did not significantly increase titers, indicating upstream steps limited flux more strongly.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion notes NADPH availability may influence TR1 performance.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylpropanoid biosynthesis coupled to tropane alkaloid derivatization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cinnamate supply module for cinnamoyltropine production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates phenylalanine to form trans-cinnamic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-canonical tropane ester and demonstration product for TA derivative discovery platform.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-DO + 2% dextrose + 5% glycerol at 25 °C for 72 h
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables de novo cinnamate formation for downstream cinnamoyltropine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in tropine-producing strain; LC-MS/MS detection of cinnamoyltropine; substrate feeding comparisons
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful precursor-supply module for generating novel acylated tropane derivatives in microbial hosts.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtPAL1 supplied cinnamic acid from phenylalanine in yeast. 2. Together with At4CL5 and EcCS, it enabled de novo production of cinnamoyltropine. 3. This module expanded the platform beyond canonical medicinal TAs.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used specifically for non-canonical product generation rather than core tropine pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: phenylpropanoid biosynthesis coupled to tropane alkaloid derivatization
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: cinnamoyl-CoA supply module for cinnamoyltropine production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates cinnamic acid to cinnamoyl-CoA for use by EcCS.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-canonical tropane ester and demonstration product for derivative discovery.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-DO + 2% dextrose + 5% glycerol at 25 °C for 72 h
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides activated cinnamoyl-CoA donor for EcCS-dependent cinnamoyltropine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in yeast; product detection by LC-MS/MS and MS/MS; substrate feeding experiments
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful acyl-donor activation module for combinatorial biosynthesis of non-canonical tropane esters.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. At4CL5 activated cinnamate to cinnamoyl-CoA in yeast. 2. High-copy expression was used to support flux into EcCS. 3. It was essential for de novo cinnamoyltropine production when combined with AtPAL1 and EcCS.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Chosen because it has activity on cinnamate, unlike some 4CL enzymes with narrower specificity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the cinnamoyl group from cinnamoyl-CoA to tropine to form cinnamoyltropine.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-canonical tropane ester and proof-of-concept derivative for drug discovery.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not explicitly established, but high-copy expression used to compensate for low activity on non-native substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: heterologous constitutive expression in engineered yeast
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: YNB-DO + 2% dextrose + 5% glycerol at 25 °C for 72 h
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms the non-canonical TA cinnamoyltropine in engineered yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression; LC-MS/MS quantification; MS/MS fragmentation matching authentic standard; substrate feeding experiments
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low activity on non-native substrates required high-copy expression.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong tool for combinatorial biosynthesis of novel tropane esters by pairing with alternative acyl-donor pathways.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcCS enabled coupling of tropine with cinnamoyl-CoA to produce cinnamoyltropine de novo. 2. Product identity was validated by MS/MS against a standard. 3. This demonstrated pathway modularity across distant plant lineages for TA diversification.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used to create a non-canonical product not typically found in medicinal TA-producing Solanaceae.
